## Plan Summary

I found the bug. Here's the complete picture:

### The Bug — `src/utils.ts`, line 7

```typescript
// ❌ Current (buggy)
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

// ✅ Fix
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The `- 1` in the loop condition is an off-by-one error that causes the loop to stop **one index before the last element**, so it's never processed.

**Impact by array size:**
| Input | Expected | Actual (buggy) |
|---|---|---|
| `["hello", "world", "foo"]` | `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]` | `["HELLO", "WORLD"]` |
| `["test"]` | `["TEST"]` | `[]` |
| `["a", "b"]` | `["A", "B"]` | `["A"]` |
| `[]` | `[]` | `[]` ✓ |

### Fix
- **1 file, 1 character change**: remove `- 1` from `src/utils.ts` line 7.

### Tests
The existing test suite in `tests/utils.test.ts` already covers all the failing cases — no new tests needed. After the fix, all 6 tests should pass (`npx vitest run`).
